generatedSentence: Dr. Kael, an unlikeable scientist, maintained absolute uptime efficiency for his remote home, many kilometers from any national park or outdoors. He often revisited a dusty drawer holding a sacred black keycard, a principle from his boyhood he intended to embody. His only links were to randoms on a private website for flicks, but he dreamed to revamp his life, nevertheless, his social allergy persisted. Suddenly, a lurker sent blackmail links, threatening to impeach him for a deadly, uncontrollable viper he kept. Panic set in; a jackknife principle meant he’d vanquish this opponent, not barter for grace. He knew the anonymous whistleblower planned to amplify his mutiny, a true anarchist aiming to wrangle the structure he built, potentially sending him into abject decay. The keycard, which the lurker somehow knew about, embodied a hidden structure. The scientist, with a feeling of dread, drove his ancient gear to a nearby solarium, feeling incredibly awkward, like jockeys on wuthering jumpers. He found the veiled gang, whose leader, an absolute keeper, presented the final verdict: prove his innocence or face torture and the national guard. The truth was an illusion; he had to identify the actual buyer. He randomly noticed a bivalve shell with a flower, then exactly understood: the kingfish, a gambler from his past, was behind it. Driving home, he passed a brook, an epiphany striking him: the lottery winner had put him on a piggyback order. He offered a toast to his future, now free from cobwebs and the need to justify. No objection, no exception, just the quiet triumph of navigation; the nutcracker had been cracked.
panelSentences: Dr. Kael, an unlikeable scientist, maintained absolute uptime efficiency for his remote home, many kilometers from any national park or outdoors. He often revisited a dusty drawer holding a sacred black keycard, a principle from his boyhood he intended to embody. His only links were to randoms on a private website for flicks, but he dreamed to revamp his life, nevertheless, his social allergy persisted.

Suddenly, a lurker sent blackmail links, threatening to impeach him for a deadly, uncontrollable viper he kept. Panic set in; a jackknife principle meant he’d vanquish this opponent, not barter for grace. He knew the anonymous whistleblower planned to amplify his mutiny, a true anarchist aiming to wrangle the structure he built, potentially sending him into abject decay.

The keycard, which the lurker somehow knew about, embodied a hidden structure. The scientist, with a feeling of dread, drove his ancient gear to a nearby solarium, feeling incredibly awkward, like jockeys on wuthering jumpers. He found the veiled gang, whose leader, an absolute keeper, presented the final verdict: prove his innocence or face torture and the national guard. The truth was an illusion; he had to identify the actual buyer.

He randomly noticed a bivalve shell with a flower, then exactly understood: the kingfish, a gambler from his past, was behind it. Driving home, he passed a brook, an epiphany striking him: the lottery winner had put him on a piggyback order. He offered a toast to his future, now free from cobwebs and the need to justify. No objection, no exception, just the quiet triumph of navigation; the nutcracker had been cracked.
rawPanelSentences: Dr. Kael, an unlikeable scientist, maintained absolute uptime efficiency for his remote home, many kilometers from any national park or outdoors. He often revisited a dusty drawer holding a sacred black keycard, a principle from his boyhood he intended to embody. His only links were to randoms on a private website for flicks, but he dreamed to revamp his life, nevertheless, his social allergy persisted.

Suddenly, a lurker sent blackmail links, threatening to impeach him for a deadly, uncontrollable viper he kept. Panic set in; a jackknife principle meant he’d vanquish this opponent, not barter for grace. He knew the anonymous whistleblower planned to amplify his mutiny, a true anarchist aiming to wrangle the structure he built, potentially sending him into abject decay.

The keycard, which the lurker somehow knew about, embodied a hidden structure. The scientist, with a feeling of dread, drove his ancient gear to a nearby solarium, feeling incredibly awkward, like jockeys on wuthering jumpers. He found the veiled gang, whose leader, an absolute keeper, presented the final verdict: prove his innocence or face torture and the national guard. The truth was an illusion; he had to identify the actual buyer.

He randomly noticed a bivalve shell with a flower, then exactly understood: the kingfish, a gambler from his past, was behind it. Driving home, he passed a brook, an epiphany striking him: the lottery winner had put him on a piggyback order. He offered a toast to his future, now free from cobwebs and the need to justify. No objection, no exception, just the quiet triumph of navigation; the nutcracker had been cracked.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, thick white gutters, clean black borders. Protagonist: a lone figure with a cybernetic right arm and a glowing green ocular implant, wearing a black trench coat. TOP-LEFT PANEL: Protagonist, Dr. Kael, sits at a high-tech desk in a dimly lit, futuristic home office, staring intently at a holographic screen displaying complex data, an old, dusty wooden drawer slightly ajar to his side, wide shot. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: Close-up on the Protagonist's tense face, eyes wide with panic as a menacing, shadowy figure (the lurker) with glowing red eyes appears on a cracked holographic projection, an anaconda-like viper coiling around the projection, low angle. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: Protagonist, Dr. Kael, driving a sleek, retro-futuristic hovercar through a neon-soaked cityscape, looking nervously in his rear-view mirror at a pursuing police drone, medium shot. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: Protagonist, Dr. Kael, stands victorious in a bright, minimalist solarium, holding a small, glowing bivalve shell, a faint smile on his face as a large, shadowy figure (the kingfish) dissipates in the background, wide shot.

definitions.definition: To defeat, to overcome.
Any mollusc belonging to the taxonomic class Bivalvia, characterized by a shell consisting of two hinged sections, such as a scallop, clam, mussel or oyster.
A pericarp in which the seed case opens or splits into two parts or valves.
To represent in a physical or concrete form; to incarnate or personify.
definitions.example: As the car salesman approached, wearing a plaid suit and slicked-back hair, he seemed to embody sleaze.

definitions.definition: To represent in some other form, such as a code of laws.
definitions.example: The US Constitution aimed to embody the ideals of diverse groups of people, from Puritans to Deists.

definitions.definition: To comprise or include as part of a cohesive whole; to be made up of.
To unite in a body or mass.
(official BIPM spelling, Australian, New Zealand, Irish, Indian and South African spelling) An SI unit of length equal to 103 metres. Symbol: km
A usually plastic card which stores a digital signature that is used to operate an electronic access control lock.
Fine particles
The act of cleaning by dusting.
The earth, as the resting place of the dead.
The earthy remains of bodies once alive; the remains of the human body.
Something worthless.
A low or mean condition.
Cash; money (in reference to gold dust).
A disturbance or uproar.
definitions.example: to raise, or kick up, a dust

definitions.definition: A totally disconnected set of points with a fractal structure.
To remove dust from.
definitions.example: The cleaning lady needs a stool to dust the cupboard.

definitions.definition: To remove dust; to clean by removing dust.
definitions.example: Dusting always makes me cough.

definitions.definition: Of a bird, to cover itself in sand or dry, dusty earth.
To spray or cover something with fine powder or liquid.
definitions.example: The mother dusted her baby's bum with talcum powder.

definitions.definition: To leave; to rush off.
To reduce to a fine powder; to levigate.
To kill or severely disable.
An open-topped box that can be slid in and out of the cabinet that contains it, used for storing clothing or other articles.
A side panel containing supplementary content.
Not likable
To move out of a dwelling, either by choice or by eviction.
definitions.example: I have to vacate my house by midday, as the new owner is moving in.

definitions.definition: To leave an office or position.
definitions.example: He vacated his coaching position because of the corruption scandal.

definitions.definition: To have a court judgement set aside; to annul.
definitions.example: The judge vacated the earlier decision when new evidence was presented.

definitions.definition: To leave an area, usually as a result of orders from public authorities in the event of a riot or natural disaster.
definitions.example: If you do not immediately vacate the area, we will make you leave with tear gas!

definitions.definition: Men's or boys' briefs.
The extent to which time is well used for the intended task.
definitions.example: The efficiency of the planning department is deplorable.

definitions.definition: The quality of producing an effect or effects.
The extent to which a resource, such as electricity, is used for the intended purpose; the ratio of useful work to energy expended.
definitions.example: The efficiency of this loudspeaker is 40%.

definitions.definition: A one-room apartment.
definitions.example: I have an efficiency available June through July.

definitions.definition: (often preceded by "the") The environment outside of enclosed structures.
(often preceded by "the") The natural environment in the open air, countryside away from cities and buildings.
definitions.example: She loves the outdoors.

definitions.definition: Not inside a house or under covered structure; unprotected; in the open air.
definitions.example: They went outdoors to light up their cigarettes.

definitions.definition: A subject of a nation.
definitions.example: The diplomats were advised not to interact with any foreign nationals except on official duty.

definitions.definition: (usually in the plural) A tournament in which participants from all over the nation compete.
definitions.example: After winning the regional tournament, the team advanced to the nationals.

definitions.definition: Of or having to do with a nation.
(by extension) Of or having to do with a country (sovereign state).
definitions.example: Import tariffs were raised for the national interest.

definitions.definition: The extortion of money by threats of public accusation, exposure, or censure.
A form of protection money (or corn, cattle, etc.) anciently paid, in the north of England and south of Scotland, to the allies of robbers in order to be spared from pillage.
Black rent, or rent paid in corn, meat, or the lowest coin, as opposed to white rent, which was paid in silver.
Compromising material that can be used to extort someone, dirt.
To extort money or favors from (a person) by exciting fears of injury other than bodily harm, such as injury to reputation, distress of mind, false accusation, etc.
definitions.example: He blackmailed a businesswoman by threatening to expose an alleged fraud.

definitions.definition: (Kenya) To speak ill of someone; to defame someone.
Some text or a graphic in an electronic document that can be activated to display another document or trigger an action.
definitions.example: Click the hyperlink to go to the next page.

definitions.definition: (by extension) An address, URL, or program that defines a hyperlink's function.
definitions.example: Copy the hyperlink and paste it into an email.

definitions.definition: A connection between places, people, events, things, or ideas.
definitions.example: The mayor’s assistant serves as the link to the media.

definitions.definition: One element of a chain or other connected series.
definitions.example: The third link of the silver chain needs to be resoldered.

definitions.definition: The connection between buses or systems.
definitions.example: A by-N-link is composed of N lanes.

definitions.definition: A space comprising one or more disjoint knots.
A thin wild bank of land splitting two cultivated patches and often linking two hills.
An individual person or element in a system
Anything doubled and closed like a link of a chain.
definitions.example: a link of horsehair

definitions.definition: A sausage that is not a patty.

(kinematics) Any one of the several elementary pieces of a mechanism, such as the fixed frame, or a rod, wheel, mass of confined liquid, etc., by which relative motion of other parts is produced and constrained.

Any intermediate rod or piece for transmitting force or motion, especially a short connecting rod with a bearing at each end; specifically (in steam engines) the slotted bar, or connecting piece, to the opposite ends of which the eccentric rods are jointed, and by means of which the movement of the valve is varied, in a link motion.
The length of one joint of Gunter's chain, being the hundredth part of it, or 7.92 inches, the chain being 66 feet in length.
A bond of affinity, or a unit of valence between atoms; applied to a unit of chemical force or attraction.
(plural) The windings of a river; the land along a winding stream.
To connect two or more things.
(of a Web page) To contain a hyperlink to another page.
definitions.example: My homepage links to my wife's.

definitions.definition: To supply (somebody) with a hyperlink; to direct by means of a link.
definitions.example: Haven't you seen his Web site? I'll link you to it.

definitions.definition: To post a hyperlink to.
definitions.example: Stop linking those unfunny comics all the time!

definitions.definition: To demonstrate a correlation between two things.
To combine objects generated by a compiler into a single executable.
A torch, used to light dark streets.
To skip or trip along smartly; to go quickly.
A dwelling.
One’s native land; the place or country in which one dwells; the place where one’s ancestors dwell or dwelt.
The locality where a thing is usually found, or was first found, or where it is naturally abundant; habitat; seat.
definitions.example: the home of the pine

definitions.definition: A focus point.
(of animals) To return to its owner.
definitions.example: The dog homed.

definitions.definition: (always with "in on") To seek or aim for something.
definitions.example: The missile was able to home in on the target.

definitions.definition: Of or pertaining to one’s dwelling or country; domestic; not foreign; as home manufactures; home comforts.
(except in phrases) That strikes home; direct, pointed.
Personal, intimate.
Relating to the home team (the team at whose venue a game is played).
definitions.example: the home end, home advantage, home supporters

definitions.definition: To one's home
At or in one's place of residence or one's customary or official location; at home
definitions.example: Everyone's gone to watch the game; there's nobody home.

definitions.definition: To a full and intimate degree; to the heart of the matter; fully, directly.
Into the goal
Into the right, proper or stowed position
definitions.example: sails sheeted home

definitions.definition: A directory that contains a user's files.
Intentional causing of somebody's experiencing agony
definitions.example: In every war there are acts of torture that cause the world to shudder.

definitions.definition: The "suffering of the heart" imposed by one on another, as in personal relationships
definitions.example: Coventry City midfielder Josh Ruffels described his 11 months out injured as 'absolute torture' after the goalless draw with Derby County Under-21s. (http//www.ccfc.co.uk/news/article/eleven-months-of-absolute-torture-760652.aspx)

definitions.definition: (often as "absolute torture") stage fright, severe embarrassment
To intentionally inflict severe pain or suffering on (someone).
definitions.example: In the aftermath of 9/11, we did some things that were wrong. We did a whole lot of things that were right, but, we tortured some folks. We did some things that were contrary to our values.

definitions.definition: Someone or something that jumps, e.g. a participant in a jumping event in track or skiing.
A person who attempts suicide by jumping from a great height.
A short length of electrical conductor, to make a temporary connection. Also jump wire.
A removable connecting pin on an electronic circuit board.

A long drilling tool used by masons and quarry workers, consisting of an iron bar with a chisel-edged steel tip at one or both ends, operated by striking it against the rock, turning it slightly with each blow.
A crude kind of sleigh, usually a simple box on runners which are in one piece with the poles that form the thills.
A jumping spider
The larva of the cheese fly.
(18th century) One of certain Calvinistic Methodists in Wales whose worship was characterized by violent convulsions.
A spring to impel the star wheel, or a pawl to lock fast a wheel, in a repeating timepiece.
A shot in which the player releases the ball at the highest point of a jump; a jump shot.
A nuclear power plant worker who repairs equipment in areas with extremely high levels of radiation.
A woolen sweater or pullover.
A loose outer jacket, especially one worn by workers and sailors.
A one-piece, sleeveless dress, or a skirt with straps and a complete or partial bodice, usually worn over a blouse by women and children.
(usually as jumpers) Rompers.
One who plays at a game of chance, who gambles.
One who takes significant risks.
A renovation, revision or improvement.
definitions.example: a revamp of a website

definitions.definition: To renovate, revise, improve or renew.
definitions.example: They plan to revamp the historical theater in the old downtown.

definitions.definition: Overpowering fright, often affecting groups of people or animals.
Rapid reduction in asset prices due to broad efforts to raise cash in anticipation of continuing decline in asset prices.
A kernel panic or system crash.
To feel overwhelming fear.
To cause somebody to panic.
(by extension) To crash.
(by extension) To cause the system to crash.
Pertaining to the god Pan.
Of fear, fright etc: sudden or overwhelming (attributed by the ancient Greeks to the influence of Pan).
A person other than a family member, spouse or lover whose company one enjoys and towards whom one feels affection.
definitions.example: John and I have been friends ever since we were roommates at college.   Trust is important between friends.   I used to find it hard to make friends when I was shy.

definitions.definition: An associate who provides assistance.
definitions.example: The Automobile Association is every motorist's friend.   The police is every law-abiding citizen's friend.

definitions.definition: A person with whom one is vaguely or indirectly acquainted.
definitions.example: a friend of a friend;  I added him as a friend on Facebook, but I hardly know him.

definitions.definition: A person who backs or supports something.
definitions.example: I’m not a friend of cheap wine.

definitions.definition: An object or idea that can be used for good.
definitions.example: Wiktionary is your friend.

definitions.definition: (used only in the vocative) Used as a form of address when warning someone.
definitions.example: You’d better watch it, friend.

definitions.definition: A function or class granted special access to the private and protected members of another class.
A spring-loaded camming device.
A lover; a boyfriend or girlfriend.
A relative, a relation by blood or marriage.

definitions.definition: To act as a friend to, to befriend; to be friendly to, to help.
To add (a person) to a list of friends on a social networking site; to officially designate (someone) as a friend.
A short, quick movement, especially a brush, sweep, or flip.
definitions.example: He removed the speck of dust with a flick of his finger.

definitions.definition: A motion picture; (in plural, usually preceded by "the") movie theater, cinema.
definitions.example: My all-time favorite flick is "Gone with the Wind."

definitions.definition: A cut that lands with the point, often involving a whip of the foible of the blade to strike at a concealed target.
A powerful underarm volley shot.
The act of pressing a place on a touch screen device.
A flitch.

definitions.definition: A unit of time, equal to 1/705,600,000 of a second
To move or hit (something) with a short, quick motion.
definitions.example: flick one's hair

definitions.definition: Characterized by solemn religious ceremony or religious use, especially, in a positive sense; consecrated, made holy.
definitions.example: a sacred day

definitions.definition: Religious; relating to religion, or to the services of religion; not secular
Spiritual; concerned with metaphysics.
Designated or exalted by a divine sanction; possessing the highest title to obedience, honor, reverence, or veneration; entitled to extreme reverence; venerable.
Not to be profaned or violated; inviolable.
(followed by the preposition "to") Consecrated; dedicated; devoted
Solemnly devoted, in a bad sense, as to evil, vengeance, curse, or the like; accursed; baleful.
The colour/color perceived in the absence of light, but also when no light is reflected, but rather absorbed.
A black dye or pigment.
A pen, pencil, crayon, etc., made of black pigment.
(in the plural) Black cloth hung up at funerals.
(sometimes capitalised) A person of African, Aborigine, or Maori descent; a dark-skinned person.
Blackness, the condition of having dark skin.
The black ball.
The edge of home plate.
A type of firecracker that is really more dark brown in colour.
Blackcurrant syrup (in mixed drinks, e.g. snakebite and black, cider and black).
(in chess and similar games) The person playing with the black set of pieces.
definitions.example: At this point black makes a disastrous move.

definitions.definition: Something, or a part of a thing, which is black.
A stain; a spot.
A dark smut fungus, harmful to wheat.
Marijuana.
To make black; to blacken.
To apply blacking to (something).
To boycott, usually as part of an industrial dispute.
(of an object) Absorbing all light and reflecting none; dark and hueless.
(of a place, etc) Without light.
(sometimes capitalized) Of or relating to any of various ethnic groups having dark pigmentation of the skin.
Designated for use by those ethnic groups which have dark pigmentation of the skin.
definitions.example: black drinking fountain; black hospital

definitions.definition: (of a card) Of the spades or clubs suits. Compare red
definitions.example: I was dealt two red queens, and he got one of the black queens.

definitions.definition: Bad; evil; ill-omened.
Expressing menace, or discontent; threatening; sullen.
definitions.example: He shot her a black look.

definitions.definition: (of objects, markets, etc) Illegitimate, illegal or disgraced.
Overcrowded.
(of coffee or tea) Without any cream, milk or creamer.
definitions.example: Jim drinks his coffee black, but Ellen prefers it with creamer.

definitions.definition: Of or relating to the playing pieces of a board game deemed to belong to the "black" set (in chess the set used by the player who moves second) (often regardless of the pieces' actual colour).
Said of a symbol or character that is solid, filled with color. Compare white.
Related to the Christian Democratic Union of Germany.
definitions.example: After the election, the parties united in a black-yellow alliance.

definitions.definition: Clandestine; relating to a political, military, or espionage operation or site, the existence or details of which is withheld from the general public.
definitions.example: 5 percent of the Defense Department funding will go to black projects.

definitions.definition: Occult; relating to something (such as mystical or magical knowledge) which is unknown to or kept secret from the general public.
Protestant, often with the implication of being militantly pro-British or anti-Catholic
Having one or more features (hair, fur, armour, clothes, bark, etc) that is dark (or black); in taxonomy, especially: dark in comparison to another species with the same base name.
Foul; dirty.
To make a rushing sound; to whizz.
To shake vigorously.
(1950s slang) A teenager who imitated the beatniks.

(1960s slang; still widely used in reference to that era) One who chooses not to conform to prevailing social norms: especially one who subscribes to values or actions such as acceptance or self-practice of recreational drug use, liberal or radical sexual mores, advocacy of communal living, strong pacifism or anti-war sentiment, etc.
(modern slang) A person who keeps an unkempt or sloppy appearance and wearing unusually long hair (for males), and because of it, often stereotyped as a deadbeat.
Someone who dresses in a hippie style.
One who is hip.
A convulsive fit of coughing or laughter; a sonorous indraft of breath; a whoop; a gasp of breath caused by laughing, coughing, or crying.
To laugh loudly.
To gasp for breath as in a severe fit of coughing.
A tight curl, twist, or bend in a length of thin material, hair etc.
definitions.example: We couldn't get enough water to put out the fire because of a kink in the hose.

definitions.definition: A difficulty or flaw that is likely to impede operation, as in a plan or system.
definitions.example: They had planned to open another shop downtown, but their plan had a few kinks.

definitions.definition: An unreasonable notion; a crotchet; a whim; a caprice.
Peculiarity or deviation in sexual behaviour or taste.
A positive 1-soliton solution to the Sine–Gordon equation
To form a kink or twist.
To be formed into a kink or twist.
The grounds around a school.
An open field near a school used for recreation by the students.
An implement for cracking nuts.
Either of two birds of the genus Nucifraga in the crow family.
A bootleg mixed drink made from a blend of alcohols and fruit juices.
A compact folding knife.
definitions.example: He kept a jack-knife in his pocket for various tasks.

definitions.definition: The front-dive pike, in which the body folds and unfolds.
definitions.example: It took me hundreds of dives to master even the simple jackknife

definitions.definition: A semi-trailer truck accident in which the vehicle mimics the closing of a jack-knife.
definitions.example: I have seen several jack-knives along that dangerous stretch of road.

definitions.definition: To fold in the middle, as a jackknife does.
definitions.example: The cat jackknifed in the air and landed gracefully on its feet.

definitions.definition: To cause a semi-trailer truck to fold like a jackknife in a traffic accident.
definitions.example: Before I knew what was happening, I'd jack-knifed the truck.

definitions.definition: A resampling method that applies estimators to all subsamples that each omit a single different group (possibly of a single datapoint) of the original sample to provide a sample distribution of the estimate.
A fundamental assumption or guiding belief.
definitions.example: We need some sort of principles to reason from.

definitions.definition: A rule used to choose among solutions to a problem.
definitions.example: The principle of least privilege holds that a process should only receive the permissions it needs.

definitions.definition: (sometimes pluralized) Moral rule or aspect.
definitions.example: I don't doubt your principles.

definitions.definition: A rule or law of nature, or the basic idea on how the laws of nature are applied.
definitions.example: Bernoulli's Principle

definitions.definition: A fundamental essence, particularly one producing a given quality.
definitions.example: Many believe that life is the result of some vital principle.

definitions.definition: A beginning.
A source, or origin; that from which anything proceeds; fundamental substance or energy; primordial substance; ultimate element, or cause.
An original faculty or endowment.
To equip with principles; to establish, or fix, in certain principles; to impress with any tenet or rule of conduct.
One who lurks.
A small fishing-boat.
Old furniture or other items that take up room, or are stored away.
Useless or cumbrous material.
A pawnbroker's shop, or room for storing articles put in pawn; hence, a pledge, or pawn.
Wood sawn into planks or otherwise prepared for sale or use, especially as a building material.
A baseball bat.
To move clumsily and heavily; to move slowly.
(with with) To load down with things, to fill, to encumber, to impose an unwanted burden on
definitions.example: I got lumbered with that boring woman all afternoon.

definitions.definition: To heap together in disorder.
To fill or encumber with lumber.
definitions.example: to lumber up a room

definitions.definition: The visible part of fire; a stream of burning vapour or gas, emitting light and heat.
A romantic partner or lover in a usually short-lived but passionate affair.
Intentionally insulting criticism or remark meant to incite anger.
A brilliant reddish orange-gold fiery colour.
The contrasting light and dark figure seen in wood used for stringed instrument making; the curl.
definitions.example: The cello has a two-piece back with a beautiful narrow flame.

definitions.definition: Burning zeal, passion, imagination, excitement, or anger.
To produce flames; to burn with a flame or blaze.
To burst forth like flame; to break out in violence of passion; to be kindled with zeal or ardour.
To post a destructively critical or abusive message (to somebody).
definitions.example: I flamed him for spamming in my favourite newsgroup.

definitions.definition: The process or result of being gradually decomposed.
A deterioration of condition; loss of status or fortune.
To deteriorate, to get worse, to lose strength or health, to decline in quality.
definitions.example: The pair loved to take pictures in the decaying hospital on forty-third street.

definitions.definition: (of organic material) To rot, to go bad.
definitions.example: The cat's body decayed rapidly.

definitions.definition: (of an unstable atom) To change by undergoing fission, by emitting radiation, or by capturing or losing one or more electrons.
(of a quantum system) To undergo optical decay, that is, to relax to a less excited state, usually by emitting a photon or phonon.
Loss of airspeed due to drag.
To cause to rot or deteriorate.
definitions.example: The extreme humidity decayed the wooden sculptures in the museum's collection in a matter of years.

definitions.definition: A musical instrument of the brass family, generally tuned to the key of B-flat; by extension, any type of lip-vibrated aerophone, most often valveless and not chromatic.
definitions.example: The royal herald sounded a trumpet to announce their arrival.

definitions.definition: Someone who plays the trumpet; a trumpeter.
definitions.example: The trumpets were assigned to stand at the rear of the orchestra pit.

definitions.definition: The cry of an elephant, or any similar loud cry.
definitions.example: The large bull gave a basso trumpet as he charged the hunters.

definitions.definition: One who praises, or propagates praise, or is the instrument of propagating it.
A funnel, or short flaring pipe, used as a guide or conductor, as for yarn in a knitting machine.
A kind of traffic interchange involving at least one loop ramp connecting traffic either entering or leaving the terminating expressway with the far lanes of the continuous highway.
A powerful reed stop in organs, having a trumpet-like sound.
To sound loudly, be amplified
definitions.example: The music trumpeted from the speakers, hurting my ears.

definitions.definition: To play the trumpet.
definitions.example: Cedric made a living trumpeting for the change of passersby in the subway.

definitions.definition: Of an elephant, to make its cry.
definitions.example: The circus trainer cracked the whip, signaling the elephant to trumpet.

definitions.definition: To give a loud cry like that of an elephant.
To proclaim loudly; to promote enthusiastically
definitions.example: Andy trumpeted Jane's secret across the school, much to her embarrassment.

definitions.definition: To render larger, more extended, or more intense.
definitions.example: amplify a microscopes

definitions.definition: To enlarge by addition or commenting; to treat copiously by adding particulars, illustrations, etc.; to expand.
To increase the amplitude of something, especially of an electric current.
definitions.example: amplify a signal

definitions.definition: To add content that is not present in the source text to the target text, usually to improve the fluency of the translation.
A venomous snake in the family Viperidae.
A dangerous, treacherous, or malignant person.
A person who smokes marijuana.
To get something stuck in a confined space.
definitions.example: Her poor little baby toe got jammed in the door.

definitions.definition: To brusquely force something into a space; cram, squeeze.
definitions.example: The rush-hour train was jammed with commuters.

definitions.definition: To cause congestion or blockage. Often used with "up"
definitions.example: A single accident can jam the roads for hours.

definitions.definition: To block or confuse a broadcast signal.
To throw a pitch at or near the batter's hands.
definitions.example: Jones was jammed by the pitch.

definitions.definition: To play music (especially improvisation as a group, or an informal unrehearsed session).
To injure a finger or toe by sudden compression of the digit's tip.
definitions.example: When he tripped on the step he jammed his toe.

definitions.definition: To attempt to score points.
definitions.example: Toughie jammed four times in the second period.

definitions.definition: To bring (a vessel) so close to the wind that half her upper sails are laid aback.
To give up on a date or some joint endeavour; stand up, chicken out, jam out.
Stuck.
definitions.example: How to treat a jammed finger or toe.

definitions.definition: Overcrowded; congested
definitions.example: A jammed Oktoberfest beer tent.

definitions.definition: A roving motion; course without definite direction; lack of rule or method; chance.
Speed, full speed; impetuosity, force.
The full range of a bullet or other projectile; hence, the angle at which a weapon is tilted to allow the greatest range.
An undefined, unknown or unimportant person; a person of no consequence.
definitions.example: The party was boring. It was full of randoms.

definitions.definition: The direction of a rake-vein.
A collection of interlinked web pages on the World Wide Web that are typically accessible from the same base URL and reside on the same server.
One who keeps something.
definitions.example: Finders keepers; losers weepers].

definitions.definition: A person or thing worth keeping.
A person charged with guarding or caring for, storing, or maintaining something; a custodian, a guard; sometimes a gamekeeper.
The player charged with guarding a goal or wicket. Short form of goalkeeper, wicketkeeper.
A part of a mechanism that catches or retains another part, for example the part of a door lock that fits in the frame and receives the bolt.
An offensive play in which the quarterback runs toward the goal with the ball after it is snapped.
One who remains or keeps in a place or position.
A fruit or vegetable that keeps for some time without spoiling.
Subject to death; mortal.
Causing death; lethal.
Aiming or willing to destroy; implacable; desperately hostile.
definitions.example: deadly enemies

definitions.definition: Very accurate (of aiming with a bow, firearm, etc.).
Very boring.
Excellent, awesome, cool.
Fatally, mortally.
In a way which suggests death.
definitions.example: Her face suddenly became deadly white.

definitions.definition: Extremely.
Not able to be controlled, contained or governed.
definitions.example: She got the uncontrollable urge for some chocolate.

definitions.definition: That which is independent of context-dependent interpretation, inviolate, fundamental.
definitions.example: moral absolutes

definitions.definition: Anything that is absolute.
In a plane, the two imaginary circular points at infinity; in space of three dimensions, the imaginary circle at infinity.
(usually capitalized) A realm which exists without reference to anything else; that which can be imagined purely by itself; absolute ego.
(usually capitalized) The unity of spirit and nature; God.
(usually capitalized) The whole of reality; the totality to which everything is reduced.
A concentrated natural flower oil, used for perfumes; an alcoholic extract of a concrete.
Free of restrictions, limitations, qualifications or conditions; unconditional.
definitions.example: 1658, Samuel Hoard, God[']s Love to Mankind, Manifested, by disprooving his absolute decree for their damnation

definitions.definition: Free from imperfection, perfect, complete; especially, perfectly embodying a quality in its essential characteristics or to its highest degree.
definitions.example: absolute purity, absolute liberty

definitions.definition: Pure, free from mixture or adulteration; unmixed.
definitions.example: absolute alcohol

definitions.definition: Complete, utter, outright; unmitigated, not qualified or diminished in any way.
definitions.example: When caught, he told an absolute lie.   an absolute denial of all charges

definitions.definition: Positive, certain; unquestionable.
Certain; free from doubt or uncertainty (e.g. a person, opinion or prediction).
Fundamental, ultimate, intrinsic; not relative; independent of references or relations to other things or standards.
definitions.example: Absolute rights and duties are such as pertain to man in a state of nature as contradistinguished from relative rights and duties, or such as pertain to him in his social relations.

definitions.definition: Independent of arbitrary units of measurement, standards, or properties; not comparative or relative.
definitions.example: absolute velocity, absolute motion, absolute position

definitions.definition: (grammar) Not immediately dependent on the other parts of the sentence; not in a syntactical relation with other parts of a text, or qualifying the text as a whole rather than any single word in it, like "it being over" in "it being over, she left".
As measured using an absolute value.
definitions.example: absolute deviation

definitions.definition: Indicating an expression that is true for all real numbers, or of all values of the variable; unconditional.
Pertaining to a grading system based on the knowledge of the individual and not on the comparative knowledge of the group of students.
Independent of (references to) other arts; expressing things (beauty, ideas, etc) only in one art.
definitions.example: absolute music

definitions.definition: Absolved; free.

A disorder of the immune system causing adverse reactions to substances (allergens) not harmful to most and marked by the body's production of histamines and associated with atopy, anaphylaxis, and asthma.
Any condition of hypersensitivity to a substance.
Altered susceptibility to a first treatment as exhibited in reaction to a subsequent one.
An antipathy, as toward a person or activity.
A decision on an issue of fact in a civil or criminal case or an inquest.
definitions.example: The jury returned a “not guilty” verdict.

definitions.definition: An opinion or judgement.
definitions.example: a “not out” verdict from the umpire

definitions.definition: An exchange of goods or services without the use of money.
definitions.example: We had no money so we had to live by barter.

definitions.definition: The goods or services used in such an exchange.
definitions.example: The man used his watch as barter to pay for his tab.

definitions.definition: To exchange goods or services without involving money.
definitions.example: She bartered a bonsai for one of the rare books in my library.

definitions.definition: An act of wrangling.
definitions.example: Wrangle and bloodshed followed thence.

definitions.definition: An angry dispute.
To bicker, or quarrel angrily and noisily.
To herd (horses or other livestock); to supervise, manage (people).
To involve in a quarrel or dispute; to embroil.
The period of time immediately following a marriage.
A trip taken by a newly married couple during this period.
definitions.example: We went to Greece for our honeymoon.

definitions.definition: A period of goodwill at the beginning of a new term or relationship (e.g. towards a newly elected politician or in respect of a new business arrangement).
definitions.example: Now that the honeymoon is over, it’s time for us to get down to the business at hand.

definitions.definition: To have a honeymoon (a trip taken by a couple after wedding).
definitions.example: My parents honeymooned at Niagara falls.

definitions.definition: A colorful, conspicuous structure associated with angiosperms, frequently scented and attracting various insects, and which may or may not be used for sexual reproduction.
A reproductive structure in angiosperms (flowering plants), often conspicuously colourful and typically including sepals, petals, and either or both stamens and/or a pistil.
A plant that bears flowers, especially a plant that is small and lacks wood.
definitions.example: We transplanted the flowers to a larger pot.

definitions.definition: (usually with in) Of plants, a state of bearing blooms.
definitions.example: The dogwoods are in flower this week.

definitions.definition: (hypocoristic) The vulva, especially the labia majora.
The best examples or representatives of a group.
definitions.example: We selected the flower of the applicants.

definitions.definition: The best state of things; the prime.
definitions.example: She was in the flower of her life.

definitions.definition: Flour.
(in the plural) A substance in the form of a powder, especially when condensed from sublimation.
definitions.example: the flowers of sulphur

definitions.definition: A figure of speech; an ornament of style.
Ornamental type used chiefly for borders around pages, cards, etc.
(in the plural) Menstrual discharges.
To put forth blooms.
definitions.example: This plant flowers in June.

definitions.definition: To decorate with pictures of flowers.
To reach a state of full development or achievement.
To froth; to ferment gently, as new beer.
To come off as flowers by sublimation.
Any of several food fishes of the genus Menticirrhus from the Atlantic; kingcroaker
Other Atlantic fish
Any of several similar fishes of the Pacific.
Caranx
The cobia, Rachycentron canadum, of warm waters globally.
Any plant in the taxonomic genus Kalmia.
A cohesive whole built up of distinct parts.
definitions.example: The birds had built an amazing structure out of sticks and various discarded items.

definitions.definition: The underlying shape of a solid.
definitions.example: He studied the structure of her face.

definitions.definition: The overall form or organization of something.
definitions.example: The structure of a sentence.

definitions.definition: A set of rules defining behaviour.
definitions.example: For some, the structure of school life was oppressive.

definitions.definition: Several pieces of data treated as a unit.
definitions.example: This structure contains both date and timezone information.

definitions.definition: Underwater terrain or objects (such as a dead tree or a submerged car) that tend to attract fish
definitions.example: There's lots of structure to be fished along the west shore of the lake; the impoundment submerged a town there when it was built.

definitions.definition: A body, such as a political party, with a cohesive purpose or outlook.
definitions.example: The South African leader went off to consult with the structures.

definitions.definition: A set along with a collection of finitary functions and relations.
To give structure to; to arrange.
definitions.example: I'm trying to structure my time better so I'm not always late.

definitions.definition: A (usually marked) place where buses, trams or trains halt to let passengers get on and off, usually smaller than a station.
definitions.example: They agreed to meet at the bus stop.

definitions.definition: An action of stopping; interruption of travel.
definitions.example: That stop was not planned.

definitions.definition: That which stops, impedes, or obstructs; an obstacle; an impediment.
A device intended to block the path of a moving object
definitions.example: door stop

definitions.definition: A consonant sound in which the passage of air through the mouth is temporarily blocked by the lips, tongue, or glottis.
A symbol used for purposes of punctuation and representing a pause or separating clauses, particularly a full stop, comma, colon or semicolon.
A knob or pin used to regulate the flow of air in an organ.

definitions.definition: One of the vent-holes in a wind instrument, or the place on the wire of a stringed instrument, by the stopping or pressing of which certain notes are produced.
A very short shot which touches the ground close behind the net and is intended to bounce as little as possible.
The depression in a dog’s face between the skull and the nasal bones.
definitions.example: The stop in a bulldog's face is very marked.

definitions.definition: A part of a photographic system that reduces the amount of light.
A unit of exposure corresponding to a doubling of the brightness of an image.
An f-stop.
The diaphragm used in optical instruments to cut off the marginal portions of a beam of light passing through lenses.
A coup d'arret, or stop thrust.
To cease moving.
definitions.example: I stopped at the traffic lights.

definitions.definition: To not continue.
definitions.example: Soon the rain will stop.

definitions.definition: To cause (something) to cease moving or progressing.
definitions.example: The sight of the armed men stopped him in his tracks.

definitions.definition: To cease; to no longer continue (doing something).
definitions.example: One of the wrestlers suddenly stopped fighting.

definitions.definition: To cause (something) to come to an end.
definitions.example: The referees stopped the fight.

definitions.definition: To close or block an opening.
definitions.example: He stopped the wound with gauze.

definitions.definition: (often with "up" or "down") To adjust the aperture of a camera lens.
definitions.example: To achieve maximum depth of field, he stopped down to an f-stop of 22.

definitions.definition: To stay; to spend a short time; to reside or tarry temporarily.
definitions.example: He stopped at his friend's house before continuing with his drive.

definitions.definition: To regulate the sounds of (musical strings, etc.) by pressing them against the fingerboard with the finger, or otherwise shortening the vibrating part.
To punctuate.
To make fast; to stopper.
One who believes in or advocates the absence of hierarchy and authority in most forms (compare anarchism), especially one who works toward the realization of such.
One who disregards laws and social norms as a form of rebellion against authority.
(by extension) One who promotes chaos and lawlessness; a nihilist.
One who resents outside control or influence on his or her life, in particular a government, and therefore desires the absence of political control.
(somewhat rare) Relating to anarchism or to anarchists, anarchistic.
A person in the lowest and most despicable condition; a castaway; outcast.
Sunk to or existing in a low condition, state, or position.
Cast down in spirit or hope; degraded; servile; grovelling; despicable; lacking courage; offered in a humble and often ingratiating spirit.
Showing utter hopelessness, helplessness; showing resignation; wretched.
Rejected; cast aside.
In spite of what preceded; yet.
A meal eaten later in the day than breakfast and earlier than lunch, and often consisting of typical foods from both of those meals.
To eat brunch.
The state, quality, or condition of being twisted, physically or mentally:
A distortion:
The threads that run lengthwise in a woven fabric; crossed by the woof or weft.
The foundation, the basis, the undergirding.
A line or cable or rode as is used in warping (mooring or hauling) a ship, and sometimes for other purposes such as deploying a seine or creating drag.
A theoretical construct that permits travel across a medium without passing through it normally, such as a teleporter or time warp.
A situation or place which is or seems to be from another era; a time warp.
The sediment which subsides from turbid water; the alluvial deposit of muddy water artificially introduced into low lands in order to enrich or fertilise them.
(obsolete outside dialectal) A throw or cast, as of fish (in which case it is used as a unit of measure: about four fish, though sometimes three or even two), oysters, etc.
definitions.example: a warp of fish

definitions.definition: Large movable item(s), usually in a room, which enhance(s) the room's characteristics, functionally or decoratively.
definitions.example: A chair is furniture. Sofas are also furniture.

definitions.definition: The harness, trappings etc. of a horse, hawk, or other animal.
Fittings, such as handles, of a door, coffin, or other wooden item.
The stock and forearm of a weapon.
The pieces of wood or metal put round pages of type to make proper margins and fill the spaces between the pages and the chase.
Any material on the page other than the text and pictures of stories.
To establish the identity of someone or something.
To disclose the identity of someone.
definitions.example: The Associated Press will not identify the suspect of the crime because he is a juvenile.

definitions.definition: To establish the taxonomic classification of an organism.
To equate or make the same; to unite or combine into one.
To have a strong affinity with; to feel oneself to be modelled on or connected to.
To associate oneself with some group.
To claim an identity; to describe oneself as a member of a group; to assert the use of a particular term to describe oneself.
To hinder, impede, or prevent.
To bring a legal proceeding against a public official.
definitions.example: President Clinton was impeached by the House in November 1999, but since the Senate acquitted him, he was not removed from office.

definitions.definition: To charge with impropriety; to discredit; to call into question.
To demonstrate in court that a testimony under oath contradicts another testimony from the same person, usually one taken during deposition.
The period of time a computer has functioned since last requiring a reboot.

One whose activities make use of the scientific method to answer questions regarding the measurable universe. A scientist may be involved in original research, or make use of the results of the research of others.
Someone or something that is awkward.
Lacking dexterity in the use of the hands, or of instruments.
definitions.example: John was awkward at performing the trick. He'll have to practice to improve.

definitions.definition: Not easily managed or effected; embarrassing.
definitions.example: An awkward silence had fallen.

definitions.definition: Lacking social skills, or uncomfortable with social interaction.
definitions.example: I'm very awkward at parties.

definitions.definition: Perverse; adverse; difficult to handle.
definitions.example: He's a right awkward chap.

definitions.definition: In a backwards direction.
Equipment or paraphernalia, especially that used for an athletic endeavor.
Clothing; garments.
Goods; property; household items.
A wheel with grooves (teeth) engraved on the outer circumference, such that two such devices can interlock and convey motion from one to the other; a gear wheel.
A particular combination or choice of interlocking gears, such that a particular gear ratio is achieved.
A configuration of the transmission of a motor car so as to achieve a particular ratio of engine to axle torque.
Recreational drugs, including steroids.
Stuff.
Business matters; affairs; concern.
Anything worthless; nonsense; rubbish.
To provide with gearing; to fit with gears in order to achieve a desired gear ratio.
To be in, or come into, gear.
To dress; to put gear on; to harness.
(usually with to or toward(s)) To design or devise (something) so as to be suitable (for a particular type of person or a particular purpose).
definitions.example: They have geared the hotel mainly at tourists.

definitions.definition: To borrow money in order to invest it in assets.
Great or fantastic
In a random manner.
By random access; at any point at a given time; not sequentially.
One who rides or travels in a vehicle, but who does not operate it and is not a member of the crew.
definitions.example: Somebody in a team who does not do their fair share of the work.

definitions.definition: A young hunting bird that can fly and is taken while it is still in its first year.
A passer-by; a wayfarer.
To ride as a passenger in a vehicle.
The state or period of being a boy.
A rough calculation or assessment of the value, size, or cost of something.
(construction and business) A document (or verbal notification) specifying how much a job is likely to cost.
An upper limitation on some positive quantity.
To calculate roughly, often from imperfect data.
To judge and form an opinion of the value of, from imperfect data.
Anything that seems to be something that it is not.
definitions.example: Using artificial additives, scientists can create the illusion of fruit flavours in food.

definitions.definition: A misapprehension; a belief in something that is in fact not true.
definitions.example: Jane has this illusion that John is in love with her.

definitions.definition: A magician’s trick.
The state of being deceived or misled.
One who opposes another; one who works or takes a position against someone or something; one who attempts to stop the progress of someone or something.
definitions.example: During the crackdown, many opponents of the regime were arrested.

definitions.definition: Opposing; adverse; antagonistic.
Situated in front; opposite.
Being everywhere at once: omnipresent.
definitions.example: To Hindus, Jews, Christians, and Muslims, God is ubiquitous.

definitions.definition: Appearing to be everywhere at once; being or seeming to be in more than one location at the same time.
Widespread; very prevalent.
Not noticed.
(money) A piece of currency, usually metallic and in the shape of a disc, but sometimes polygonal, or with a hole in the middle.
A token used in a special establishment like a casino.
That which serves for payment or recompense.
Money in general, not limited to coins.

definitions.definition: One of the suits of minor arcana in tarot, or a card of that suit.
A corner or external angle.
A small circular slice of food.
A cryptocurrency.
definitions.example: What's the best coin to buy right now?

definitions.definition: To make of a definite fineness, and convert into coins, as a mass of metal.
definitions.example: to coin a medal

definitions.definition: (by extension) To make or fabricate.
definitions.example: Over the last century the advance in science has led to many new words being coined.

definitions.definition: To acquire rapidly, as money; to make.
A spiderweb, or the remains of one, especially an asymmetrical one that is woven with an irregular pattern of threads.
One of its filaments; gossamer
Something thin and unsubstantial, or flimsy and worthless; valueless remainder.
An intricate plot to catch the unwary
A web page that either has not been updated for a long time, or that is rarely visited
The European spotted flycatcher, Muscicapa striata.
(usually in the plural) fuzzy inexact memories
A ride on somebody's back or shoulders.
An act or instance of piggybacking.
To attach or append something to another (usually larger) object or event.

definitions.definition: To obtain a wireless internet connection by bringing one's own computer within the range of another's wireless connection without that subscriber's permission or knowledge.
To utilize "last-mile" wiring rented from a larger owner ISP by a smaller ISP.
To carry someone on the back or shoulders.
To transport (a lorry/truck) on a flatbed railway waggon
To enter a secured area at the same time along with someone having authorized access; to tailgate
On somebody's back or shoulders.

definitions.definition: Pertaining to transportation of goods where one transportation unit is carried on the back of something else. For example, a truck on a train.
On somebody's back or shoulders.

definitions.definition: To use; enjoy; have the full employment of.
To earn; deserve.
To bear; endure; support; put up with; tolerate (usually used in the negative, with an abstract noun as object).
definitions.example: I will not brook any disobedience.   I will brook no refusal.   I will brook no impertinence.

definitions.definition: (manner) without approximation; precisely.
definitions.example: He divided the coins exactly in half.

definitions.definition: (focus) Used to provide emphasis.
definitions.example: He did it that way exactly to prove the point.

definitions.definition: Signifies agreement or recognition.
definitions.example: So you're saying that we have only three days left? — Yes, exactly!

definitions.definition: An organized rebellion against a legally constituted authority, especially by seamen against their officers.
Violent commotion; tumult; strife.
To commit mutiny.
definitions.example: The crew of the Bounty mutinied because of the harsh discipline of Captain Bligh.

definitions.definition: The act of objecting.
A statement expressing opposition, or a reason or cause for expressing opposition (generally followed by the adposition to).
definitions.example: I have no objection to any person's religion.

definitions.definition: An official protest raised in a court of law during a legal trial over a violation of the rules of the court by the opposing party.
Charming, pleasing qualities.

definitions.definition: A short prayer of thanks before or after a meal.
definitions.example: It has become less common to say grace before having dinner.

definitions.definition: In the games of patience or solitaire: a special move that is normally against the rules.
A grace note.
Elegant movement; balance or poise.
definitions.example: The dancer moved with grace and strength.

definitions.definition: An allowance of time granted to a debtor during which he or she is free of at least part of his normal obligations towards the creditor.
definitions.example: The repayment of the loan starts after a three-year grace.

definitions.definition: Free and undeserved favour, especially of God; unmerited divine assistance given to humans for their regeneration or sanctification, or for resisting sin.
definitions.example: I’m so grateful to God for the grace that He has given me.

definitions.definition: An act or decree of the governing body of an English university.
To adorn; to decorate; to embellish and dignify.
definitions.example: He graced the room by simply being there.

definitions.definition: To dignify or raise by an act of favour; to honour.
To supply with heavenly grace.
To add grace notes, cadenzas, etc., to.
To provide an acceptable explanation for.

definitions.definition: To be a good, acceptable reason for; warrant.

definitions.definition: To arrange (text) on a page or a computer screen such that the left and right ends of all lines within paragraphs are aligned.
definitions.example: The text will look better justified.

definitions.definition: To absolve, and declare to be free of blame or sin.
To give reasons for one’s actions; to make an argument to prove that one is in the right.

definitions.definition: To prove; to ratify; to confirm.
To show (a person) to have had a sufficient legal reason for an act that has been made the subject of a charge or accusation.
To qualify (oneself) as a surety by taking oath to the ownership of sufficient property.
To make a sudden uncontrolled movement.
To give a quick, often unpleasant tug or shake.
To masturbate.
To beat, to hit.
To throw with a quick and suddenly arrested motion of the hand.

definitions.definition: (usually transitive) To lift using a jerk.
To flout with contempt.
To cure (meat) by cutting it into strips and drying it, originally in the sun.
A person who makes one or more purchases.
definitions.example: Every person who steps through the door is a potential buyer, so acknowledge their presence.

definitions.definition: (retailing) A person who purchases items for resale in a retail establishment.
definitions.example: The supermarket's new buyer decided to stock a larger range of vegetarian foods.

definitions.definition: A person who purchases items consumed or used as components in the manufacture of products.
The act of excepting or excluding; exclusion; restriction by taking out something which would otherwise be included, as in a class, statement, rule.
That which is excepted or taken out from others; a person, thing, or case, specified as distinct, or not included.
definitions.example: That rule is usually true, but there are a few exceptions.

definitions.definition: An objection, on legal grounds; also, as in conveyancing, a clause by which the grantor excepts or reserves something before the right is transferred.
An objection; cavil; dissent; disapprobation; offense; cause of offense; — usually followed by to or against.
An interruption in normal processing, typically caused by an error condition, that can be handled by another part of the program.
A bruise-like mark made during petting by pressing the mouth to the skin on one’s partner’s body and sucking.
An object whose name is unknown or cannot be recalled.

A printing defect caused by foreign matter on the printing surface resulting in a ring where the ink is missing, appearing as a spot of ink surrounded by a halo, or as an unprinted spot within a solid printed area.
A serif or other ornamentation on type.
Local swelling in a tissue area, especially skin, often due to injury.
A tool for making smooth, semicircular bends in conduit and pipe.
An unintended negative outcome or loss in regards to a deal or action, often preceded by the words "taking a ..." or "took a ... ".
definitions.example: Farmer Jones took a hickey on his soybean crop when the flood washed the seeds away.

definitions.definition: A scheme for the distribution of prizes by lot or chance, especially a gaming scheme in which one or more tickets bearing particular numbers draw prizes, the other tickets are blanks.
An affair of chance.
(Shakespeare) Allotment; a thing allotted.
(manner) In an incredible manner; not to be believed.
(degree) To a great extent; extremely.
definitions.example: He was incredibly wealthy.

definitions.definition: Used to note the surprising or hard-to-believe nature of what is being said and suggest that it is nevertheless true.
definitions.example: Incredibly, when he died they found he had left a million dollars to the Poetry Foundation.

definitions.definition: To provide an impetus for motion or other physical change, to move an object by means of the provision of force thereto.
definitions.example: You drive nails into wood with a hammer.

definitions.definition: To provide an impetus for a non-physical change, especially a change in one's state of mind.
definitions.example: My wife's constant harping about the condition of the house threatens to drive me to distraction.

definitions.definition: To displace either physically or non-physically, through the application of force.
To cause intrinsic motivation through the application or demonstration of force: to impel or urge onward thusly, to compel to move on, to coerce, intimidate or threaten.
(especially of animals) To impel or urge onward by force; to push forward; to compel to move on.
definitions.example: to drive twenty thousand head of cattle from Texas to the Kansas railheads; to drive sheep out of a field

definitions.definition: To direct a vehicle powered by a horse, ox or similar animal.
To cause animals to flee out of.
definitions.example: The beaters drove the brambles, causing a great rush of rabbits and other creatures.

definitions.definition: To move (something) by hitting it with great force.
definitions.example: You drive nails into wood with a hammer.

definitions.definition: To cause (a mechanism) to operate.
definitions.example: The pistons drive the crankshaft.

definitions.definition: To operate (a wheeled motorized vehicle).
definitions.example: drive a car

definitions.definition: To motivate; to provide an incentive for.
definitions.example: What drives a person to run a marathon?

definitions.definition: To compel (to do something).
definitions.example: Their debts finally drove them to sell the business.

definitions.definition: To cause to become.
definitions.example: This constant complaining is going to drive me to insanity.   You are driving me crazy!

definitions.definition: To hit the ball with a drive.
To travel by operating a wheeled motorized vehicle.
definitions.example: I drive to work every day.

definitions.definition: To convey (a person, etc) in a wheeled motorized vehicle.
definitions.example: My wife drove me to the airport.

definitions.definition: To move forcefully.
To be moved or propelled forcefully (especially of a ship).
To urge, press, or bring to a point or state.
To carry or to keep in motion; to conduct; to prosecute.
To clear, by forcing away what is contained.
To dig horizontally; to cut a horizontal gallery or tunnel.
To put together a drive (n.): to string together offensive plays and advance the ball down the field.
To distrain for rent.
To separate the lighter (feathers or down) from the heavier, by exposing them to a current of air.
To be the dominant party in a sex act.
That drives (a mechanism or process).
Of wind, rain, etc That drives forcefully; strong; forceful; violent
Arrangement, disposition, or sequence.
A position in an arrangement, disposition, or sequence.
The state of being well arranged.
definitions.example: The house is in order; the machinery is out of order.

definitions.definition: Conformity with law or decorum; freedom from disturbance; general tranquillity; public quiet.
definitions.example: to preserve order in a community or an assembly

definitions.definition: A command.
A request for some product or service; a commission to purchase, sell, or supply goods.
A group of religious adherents, especially monks or nuns, set apart within their religion by adherence to a particular rule or set of principles.
definitions.example: St. Ignatius Loyola founded the Jesuit order in 1537.

definitions.definition: An association of knights.
definitions.example: the Order of the Garter, the Order of the Bath.

definitions.definition: Any group of people with common interests.
A decoration, awarded by a government, a dynastic house, or a religious body to an individual, usually for distinguished service to a nation or to humanity.
A rank in the classification of organisms, below class and above family; a taxon at that rank.
definitions.example: Magnolias belong to the order Magnoliales.

definitions.definition: A number of things or persons arranged in a fixed or suitable place, or relative position; a rank; a row; a grade; especially, a rank or class in society; a distinct character, kind, or sort.
definitions.example: talent of a high order

definitions.definition: (chiefly plural) An ecclesiastical grade or rank, as of deacon, priest, or bishop; the office of the Christian ministry.
definitions.example: to take orders, or to take holy orders, that is, to enter some grade of the ministry

definitions.definition: The disposition of a column and its component parts, and of the entablature resting upon it, in classical architecture; hence (since the column and entablature are the characteristic features of classical architecture) a style or manner of architectural design.
The sequence in which a side’s batsmen bat; the batting order.
A power of polynomial function in an electronic circuit’s block, such as a filter, an amplifier, etc.
definitions.example: a 3-stage cascade of a 2nd-order bandpass Butterworth filter

definitions.definition: The overall power of the rate law of a chemical reaction, expressed as a polynomial function of concentrations of reactants and products.
The cardinality, or number of elements in a set, group, or other structure regardable as a set.

(of an element of a group) For given group G and element g ∈ G, the smallest positive natural number n, if it exists, such that (using multiplicative notation), gn = e, where e is the identity element of G; if no such number exists, the element is said to be of infinite order (or sometimes zero order).
The number of vertices in a graph.
A partially ordered set.
The relation on a partially ordered set that determines that it is, in fact, a partially ordered set.
The sum of the exponents on the variables in a monomial, or the highest such among all monomials in a polynomial.
definitions.example: A quadratic polynomial, a x^2 + b x + c, is said to be of order (or degree) 2.

definitions.definition: A written direction to furnish someone with money or property; compare money order, postal order.
To set in some sort of order.
To arrange, set in proper order.
To issue a command to.
definitions.example: He ordered me to leave.

definitions.definition: To request some product or service; to secure by placing an order.
definitions.example: to order groceries

definitions.definition: To admit to holy orders; to ordain; to receive into the ranks of the ministry.
Toasted bread.
definitions.example: I ate a piece of toast for breakfast.

definitions.definition: A proposed salutation (e.g. to say "cheers") while drinking alcohol.
definitions.example: At the reception, there were many toasts from the well-wishers.

definitions.definition: A person, group, or notable object to which a salutation with alcohol is made; a person or group held in similar esteem.
definitions.example: He was the toast of high society.

definitions.definition: (chiefly US) Something that will be no more; something subject to impending destruction, harm or injury.
definitions.example: If I ever get my hands on the guy that stole my wallet, he’s toast!

definitions.definition: Extemporaneous narrative poem or rap.
A transient, informational pop-up window.
An establishment with sunbeds in it or where one can rent sunbeds.
A room, with many windows, exposed to the sun.
A sundial.
To dress in, or decorate with, a veil.
To conceal as with a veil.
definitions.example: The forest fire was veiled by smoke, but I could hear it clearly.

definitions.definition: Covered by a veil.
Partly hidden or concealed.
definitions.example: His veiled threat was terrifying.

definitions.definition: To go; walk; proceed.
Adjacent, near, close by
definitions.example: He stopped at a nearby store for some groceries.

definitions.definition: Next to, close to
definitions.example: I'm glad my friends live nearby where I can visit them.

definitions.definition: The theory, practice and technology of charting a course for a ship, aircraft or spaceship or (colloquially) road vehicle.
Traffic or travel by vessel, especially commercial shipping.
A canal.
One who reports a problem or violation to the authorities; especially, an employee or former employee who reports a violation by an employer.
definitions.example: We owe it to a small group of brave whistle-blowers that we know about the infractions at all.
